title: System and method for acoustic-based location determination
abstract: The present invention enables a user to track his/her position or the position of any subject indoors. A system of ultrasonic transmitters at different points within a covered region emit ultrasonic signals that identify the transmitter from which they were sent and the times that the signals were sent. A client application located with the tracked subject receives the signals and suitable processing provides the location of the tracked subject. The processing may involve trilateralization, assumptions regarding the tracked subjects' position, and/or auxiliary information.
claims: Claims CLAIMS  We claim: 1. An acoustic-based location system comprising: at least one ultrasonic transmitter configured to transmit ultrasonic signals to an electronic communication device hosting a client application, the ultrasonic signals identifying the times the signals were sent; wherein the client application determines the location of the electronic communication device based on the location of the at least one transmitter sending the signals and the times the signals were sent. 2. The system of claim 1 , wherein the electronic communication device senses the signal strength of local Wi-Fi routers, and the client application determines the location of the electric communication device based also on the sensed signal strength of the Wi-Fi routers. 3. The system of claim 1 or 2, wherein: the at least one ultrasonic transmitter includes at least two ultrasonic transmitters, each transmitter configured to transmit ultrasonic signals to the electronic communication device; and the ultrasonic signals identify the transmitter from which they were sent. 4. The system of claim 1 , wherein the client application determines the location of the electronic device based on the ultrasonic signals of only one ultrasonic transmitter. 5. The system of any of claims 1-4 further comprising: a synchronization server configured to synchronize clocks of the ultrasonic transmitters and the client application, the clocks of the ultrasonic transmitters providing the times in which the signals were sent, and the clock of the client application providing the time in which the signals were received. 6. The system of claim 5, wherein one of the ultrasonic transmitters and the synchronization server are built as a single unit. 7. The system of any of claims 1-3, 5, and 6, wherein the client application determines the location of the electronic communication device based on the ultrasonic signals of only two ultrasonic transmitters. 8. The system of any of claims 1-7, wherein the location of the electronic communication device determined based on the signals from the ultrasonic transmitter is used to determine which messages are sent to the electronic communication device through the Internet. 9. The system of any of claims 1-8, wherein the ultrasonic transmitters communicate with each other to transfer information from which their locations are determined. 10. The system of any of claims 1 and 3-9, wherein the client application determines the location of the electronic communication device based also on Wi-Fi signal strength, the Wi-Fi signal strength being determined by the electronic communication device. 11. A method of providing information for determining location, the method comprising: transmitting a first ultrasonic signal to an electronic communication device hosting a client application, the first ultrasonic signal identifying the transmitter that sent it and the time it was sent; and transmitting a second ultrasonic signal to the electronic communication device, the second ultrasonic signal identifying the transmitter that sent it and the time it was sent; wherein the client application determines the location of the electronic communication device based on the times the ultrasonic signals were sent and on the transmitters that sent them. 12. The method of claim 11 further comprising: synchronizing clocks of the transmitters and the client application, the clocks of the transmitters providing the times in which the signals were sent, and the clock of the client application providing the time in which the signals were received. 13. The method of claims 11 or 12, wherein client application determines the location of the electronic communication device based on the ultrasonic signals of only two ultrasonic transmitters. 14. The method of any of claims 11-13 further comprising: transferring information between the transmitters to enable location determination of the transmitters. 15. The method of any of claims 11-14, wherein the client application determines the location of the electronic communication device based also on Wi-Fi signal strength, the Wi-Fi signal strength being determined by the electronic communication device. 16. A method of determining location, the method comprising: receiving a first ultrasonic signal identifying the transmitter that sent it and the time it was sent; receiving a second ultrasonic signal identifying the transmitter that sent it and the time it was sent; and determining the location where the signals were received based on the times the signals were sent and on the transmitters that sent them. 17. The method of claim 16, wherein the location is determined based on the ultrasonic signals of only two ultrasonic transmitters. 18. The method of claim 16 or 17 further comprising: determining Wi-Fi signal strength at the location where the ultrasonic signals were received; wherein the location is determined based also on Wi-Fi signal strength.
